datasets.title: Provisional Mortality Statistics
datasets.subject: Track deaths by all causes with weekly updates. Covers COVID-19, opioid overdoses, heart disease, cancer, suicide, maternal mortality, and more. The go-to dataset for recent mortality trends.
datasets.notes: Provisional — subject to revision. Updated weekly. Most current mortality data available.

datasets.title: Final Mortality, Single Race (MCD + UCD)
datasets.subject: Final (non-provisional) mortality statistics with single-race classification for 2018-2023. Includes both underlying and multiple causes of death. Use when you need finalized mortality counts with detailed race/ethnicity breakdown.
datasets.notes: Final data. Single-race classification per 1997 OMB standards.

datasets.title: Underlying Cause of Death, Single Race
datasets.subject: Final mortality data for 2018-2023 focused on underlying cause of death with single-race classification. Best for maternal mortality, cause-specific trends, and race-stratified analysis without multiple-cause filters.
datasets.notes: No MCD filters available. Use D157 if you need multiple-cause filtering.

datasets.title: Multiple Cause of Death
datasets.subject: Historical mortality with both underlying and multiple causes of death for 1999-2020. Standard dataset for drug overdose deaths, where the drug appears anywhere on the death certificate. Final, bridged-race data.
datasets.notes: Pair with D176 (2021+) for full trend since 1999. ICD-10 coding.

datasets.title: Underlying Cause of Death
datasets.subject: Historical mortality by single underlying cause of death for 1999-2020. Useful for suicide, cancer, heart disease, and cause-specific trends. Final ICD-10 data, no multiple-cause filters.
datasets.notes: No MCD (drug-involvement) filters. Use D77 for drug overdose counts.

datasets.title: Multiple Cause of Death with US-Mexico Border Regions
datasets.subject: Like D77 (MCD 1999-2020) but adds US-Mexico border region and metropolitan geography. Useful for border health, rural-urban disparities, and geographic analyses of drug or cause-specific deaths.
datasets.notes: Extends D77 with additional geographic dimensions.

datasets.title: Compressed Mortality (ICD-10)
datasets.subject: Compressed mortality data using ICD-10 codes for 1999-2016. Fewer variables than D77 but covers the same period. Useful when you need a simpler query structure for broad cause-of-death analysis.
datasets.notes: Fewer variables than D77. No MCD filters.

datasets.title: Compressed Mortality (ICD-9)
datasets.subject: Mortality using ICD-9 codes from 1979-1998. Essential for long-term trends — heart disease, cancer, HIV/AIDS, accidents — before ICD-10 was adopted in 1999.
datasets.notes: ICD-9 coding system. Pair with D77/D176 for 1979–present trends.

datasets.title: Compressed Mortality (ICD-8)
datasets.subject: Pre-ICD-9 mortality data from 1968-1978 using ICD-8 codes. For historical context: the polio era's tail, early cardiovascular mortality, before modern trauma care.
datasets.notes: ICD-8 coding. Use D16 for 1979+ comparisons.

datasets.title: Linked Birth / Infant Death Records
datasets.subject: Links birth certificates to infant death records to track neonatal and post-neonatal mortality. Covers causes of infant death, gestational age, birth weight, maternal factors, and birth outcomes.
datasets.notes: Rate = deaths per 1,000 live births. No AAR. Use D159 for expanded race/ethnicity detail.

datasets.title: Linked Birth / Infant Death Records, Expanded
datasets.subject: Like D69 but with expanded race/ethnicity detail and additional demographic variables. Best for detailed analysis of infant mortality disparities by race, Hispanic origin, and maternal characteristics.
datasets.notes: Expanded demographic detail vs D69. Only available from 2017.

datasets.title: Linked Birth / Infant Death Records (2003–2006)
datasets.subject: Linked birth/infant death data for 2003-2006. Same concept as D69 but for earlier years. Use for historical infant mortality trends.
datasets.notes: Pair with D18 (1999-2002) and D69 (2007+) for long-term trends.

datasets.title: Linked Birth / Infant Death Records (1999–2002)
datasets.subject: Linked birth/infant death data for 1999-2002.
datasets.notes: Earliest linked birth/infant death records available.

datasets.title: Linked Birth / Infant Death Records (1995–1998)
datasets.subject: Linked birth/infant death data for 1995-1998.
datasets.notes: Use with D18, D31, D69 to build 1995-present infant mortality trend.

datasets.title: Natality
datasets.subject: Live births data covering birth rates, fertility rates, maternal age, prenatal care, gestational age, birth weight, cesarean delivery, smoking during pregnancy, and other birth outcomes. The main dataset for birth statistics.
datasets.notes: No AAR. Rate = births per 1,000 population. Use D149 for expanded race detail.

datasets.title: Natality, Expanded
datasets.subject: Like D66 but with detailed single-race classification and additional demographic variables. Best for analyzing birth disparities by race and ethnicity, or when you need variables not available in D66.
datasets.notes: Expanded race detail. Only from 2016. Use D66 for 2007+ without race expansion.

datasets.title: Provisional Natality
datasets.subject: Provisional (preliminary) birth data updated monthly. Use for the most current birth trend data — national fertility rates, birth counts by state.
datasets.notes: Very limited grouping options vs D66. Use for latest provisional counts only.

datasets.title: Natality (2003–2006)
datasets.subject: Live birth data for 2003-2006. Use for historical birth trends.
datasets.notes: Pair with D66 for 2003-present trends.

datasets.title: Natality (1995–2002)
datasets.subject: Live birth data for 1995-2002. Use for historical birth trends predating D27.
datasets.notes: Earliest natality data. Use with D27 and D66 for 1995-present.

datasets.title: VAERS — Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System
datasets.subject: Reports of adverse events following vaccination from 1990 to present. Counts include deaths, hospitalizations, life-threatening reactions, and other outcomes reported after COVID-19, flu, MMR, and other vaccines. Self-reported — not evidence of causation.
key_groupings: Vaccine type (broad: COVID19, FLU, MMR, etc.)
datasets.notes: Counts REPORTS, not incidence. Not evidence of causation. Passive surveillance system — reporting is voluntary.

datasets.title: NNDSS Annual Summary Data
datasets.subject: Nationally Notifiable Disease Surveillance System (NNDSS) data on reportable infectious diseases including tick-borne diseases (Lyme, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, anaplasmosis, ehrlichiosis), STIs, measles, and more.
datasets.notes: NNDSS annual summary. Bundled query covers tick-borne diseases 2016-2023.

datasets.title: Number of Heat Wave Days
datasets.subject: Annual count of heat wave days (May-September) by county from 1981-2010. Derived from NLDAS climate data. Use to study climate-health connections and geographic heat exposure patterns.
datasets.notes: No age-adjusted rates. Geographic / temporal summary statistics.

datasets.title: NLDAS Daily Air Temperatures and Heat Index
datasets.subject: Daily minimum, mean, and maximum temperature plus heat index by county, 1979-2011, from the North American Land Data Assimilation System (NLDAS). Use for climate-health linkage studies.
datasets.notes: Statistical summaries (mean, min, max, CI). Not mortality data.

datasets.title: NLDAS Daily Sunlight
datasets.subject: Daily solar radiation (KJ/m²) by county, 1979-2011. From NLDAS. Useful for vitamin D research, seasonal affective studies, and environmental health analyses.
datasets.notes: Solar radiation data. Not mortality.

datasets.title: NLDAS Daily Precipitation
datasets.subject: Daily precipitation (mm) by county, 1979-2011. From NLDAS. Use for climate-health linkage, flooding risk analysis, or environmental exposure studies.
datasets.notes: Precipitation data. Not mortality.

datasets.title: MODIS Land Surface Temperature
datasets.subject: Satellite-derived land surface temperature by county, 2003-2008, from MODIS Terra. Use for urban heat island studies, climate-health research, and geographic thermal exposure analysis.
datasets.notes: Satellite-derived. Shorter time range than NLDAS datasets.

datasets.title: Fine Particulate Matter PM2.5
datasets.subject: Annual county-level fine particulate matter (PM2.5 µg/m³) concentrations, 2003-2011. PM2.5 is the key air pollutant linked to respiratory and cardiovascular mortality. Use to study air quality and health outcomes.
datasets.notes: Air quality data. Use with mortality data for pollution-health linkage studies.

datasets.title: US Cancer Statistics — Incidence
datasets.subject: Cancer incidence (new case counts and rates) for all cancer sites from 1999 to 2022, covering all US states plus Puerto Rico. Track breast, lung, colorectal, prostate, and 100+ other cancer types. Break down by state, age, sex, race, and year.
key_groupings: Cancer Site (all types, leading sites, childhood cancers)
datasets.notes: Most current cancer incidence. Many archived versions (D28, D40, D50, D70, D75, D85, D89, D94, D100, D113, D121, D136, D144, D151, D160, D172, D181, D187, D198) cover earlier years.

datasets.title: US Cancer Statistics — Mortality, Single Race
datasets.subject: Cancer mortality (deaths and rates) for 2018-2023 with single-race classification. Covers all cancer sites broken down by state, age, sex, and race. Pair with D205 (incidence) for survival and incidence-to-mortality comparisons.
datasets.notes: Most current cancer mortality with single-race detail. Historical archived versions: D29, D41, D51, D71, D90, D95, D101, D114, D125, D137, D145, D152, D161, D173, D182, D188, D199.

datasets.title: STD Morbidity
datasets.subject: National surveillance data on sexually transmitted infections including chlamydia, gonorrhea, syphilis (all stages), and other reportable STDs. Case counts and rates by state, year, sex, disease type, and STD surveillance region. 1984-2014.
datasets.notes: Archive data through 2014. Archived versions: D2, D36, D42, D47, D59, D86, D106. For current data, see CDC STI Surveillance Reports.

datasets.title: STD Morbidity by Age, Race, and Sex
datasets.subject: Sexually transmitted infections stratified by age group, race/ethnicity, and sex for 1996-2014. More detailed demographic breakdown than D127. Covers chlamydia, gonorrhea, and syphilis. Essential for understanding STI disparities by race and age.
datasets.notes: Archive data through 2014. Archived versions: D45, D57, D84, D107, D128.

datasets.title: Tuberculosis (OTIS TB Data)
datasets.subject: TB case surveillance from the Online Tuberculosis Information System (OTIS), 1993-2023. Track new TB cases by state, year, age, race/ethnicity, sex, origin of birth (US-born vs. foreign-born), and HIV status. Includes treatment completion and drug susceptibility testing rates.
datasets.notes: Most current TB data (2023). Annual archive versions every year from 1993: D4, D15, D26, D34, D38, D49, D55, D78, D96, D108, D123, D131, D135, D147, D156, D165, D175, D186, D193.

datasets.title: Fetal Deaths
datasets.subject: Fetal death (stillbirth) records for 2005-2024, covering 20+ weeks gestation. Track stillbirths by state, cause of death, gestational age, birth weight, delivery method, maternal age, race, and congenital anomalies (Down syndrome, anencephalus, spina bifida, omphalocele).
key_groupings: Congenital Anomalies (Down Syndrome, Spina Bifida, etc.)
datasets.notes: Use D150 for expanded version (2014+) with maternal education, tobacco, ART, and more detailed variables.

datasets.title: Fetal Deaths, Expanded
datasets.subject: Expanded fetal death (stillbirth) data for 2014-2024 with additional variables: maternal education, tobacco use during pregnancy, prenatal care timing, gestational diabetes, infertility treatments (ART, fertility drugs), prior cesarean, and detailed single-race classification.
key_groupings: Mother's Education / Nativity / Country of Birth
Cause of Death (ICD Chapter, 124 selected causes)
datasets.notes: Expanded version of D133, only from 2014. Adds ART, tobacco, education, and maternal health detail.

datasets.title: Population Estimates, Single Race
datasets.subject: Annual population estimates by state and county, 2020-2024, using single-race classification. Useful as a population denominator when calculating rates, or for studying demographic distribution by age, sex, race, and ethnicity.
datasets.notes: Denominator data. Historical bridged-race estimates (1990-2020): D1, D5, D9, D24, D32, D37, D44, D52, D82, D92, D99, D116, D129, D134, D143, D154, D163, D178. Other single-race vintages: D142, D148, D155, D164, D166-D171, D184, D185, D190, D191, D196, D197, D203.

datasets.title: Population Estimates, Bridged-Race (1990–2020)
datasets.subject: Annual population estimates using the legacy bridged-race classification (4 race categories: White, Black, AIAN, API), 1990-2020. Used as the denominator for bridged-race mortality datasets (D76, D77, D141, D176). County and state level.
datasets.notes: Legacy 4-category race system. Pairs with D76, D77, D176 mortality. Use D204 for post-2020 single-race estimates.

datasets.title: National Population Projections
datasets.subject: Census Bureau national population projections by age, sex, race, and ethnicity from 2014 to 2060. Useful for understanding long-term demographic change and projecting future healthcare demand.
datasets.notes: Projections only. Birth/death/migration components: D118 (by race), D119 (by ethnicity). State-level projections: D7.

datasets.title: AIDS Public Use Data
datasets.subject: AIDS case surveillance data from the early HIV epidemic, covering diagnoses through 1999. Demographic characteristics (age, sex, race/ethnicity) and transmission categories (MSM, IDU, heterosexual, perinatal, blood products). Historical archive of the 1980s-1990s AIDS epidemic.
datasets.notes: Historical only. Vintage 2001 (D13) and 2002 (D14) also available. Current HIV surveillance uses NHSS outside of WONDER.
